statement.markdown: Given an undirected graph represented as an adjacency list, find the shortest simple cycle. A simple cycle is a path that visits each vertex only once and returns to the starting vertex without visiting any edge more than once. Note that the graph may contain cycles of varying lengths. Output the shortest length of the simple cycle. If the graph is not connected or does not have any cycles, return -1.
statement.inputFormat: The input will consist of an integer n representing the number of nodes, followed by n - 1 edges, each edge represented as two space-separated integers.
statement.outputFormat: Output the length of the shortest simple cycle, or -1 if no simple cycle is found.
statement.notes: Use a depth-first search (DFS) approach with backtracking to find the shortest cycle.

examples.explanation: The graph contains a simple cycle: 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 1.

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int dfs(int u, int p, vector<vector<int>>& g, vector<bool>& visited, vector<int>& path) { path.push_back(u); visited[u] = true; int minPath = 99999999; for (int v : g[u]) { if (v == p) continue; if (visited[v]) { minPath = min(minPath, path.size() - 1); } else { minPath = dfs(v, u, g, visited, path); } } if (minPath != 99999999) { return minPath + 1; } return 0; } int solve(vector<vector<int>>& g) { for (int i = 0; i < g.size(); i++) { dfs(g[i][0], -1, g, vector<bool>(g.size(), false), vector<int>()); } return -1; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int dfs(int u, int p, List<List<Integer>> g, boolean[] visited, List<Integer> path) { path.add(u); visited[u] = true; int minPath = 99999999; for (int v : g.get(u)) { if (v == p) continue; if (visited[v]) { minPath = Math.min(minPath, path.size() - 1); } else { minPath = dfs(v, u, g, visited, path); } } if (minPath != 99999999) { return minPath + 1; } return 0; } public int solve(List<List<Integer>> g) { for (int i = 0; i < g.size(); i++) { dfs(g.get(i).get(0), -1, g, new boolean[g.size()], new ArrayList<Integer>()); } return -1; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def dfs(self, u, p, g, visited, path): path.append(u); visited[u] = True; minPath = 99999999; for v in g[u]: if v == p: continue; if visited[v]: minPath = min(minPath, len(path) - 1); else: minPath = self.dfs(v, u, g, visited, path); if minPath != 99999999: return minPath + 1; return 0; def solve(self, g): for i in range(len(g)): self.dfs(g[i][0], -1, g, [False for _ in range(len(g))], []); return -1;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function dfs(u, p, g, visited, path) { path.push(u); visited[u] = true; let minPath = 99999999; for (let v of g[u]) { if (v == p) continue; if (visited[v]) { minPath = Math.min(minPath, path.length - 1); } else { minPath = dfs(v, u, g, visited, path); } } if (minPath != 99999999) { return minPath + 1; } return 0; } function solve(g) { for (let i = 0; i < g.length; i++) { dfs(g[i][0], -1, g, new Array(g.length).fill(false), []); } return -1; }

solutions.python: class Solution: def dfs(self, u, p, g, visited, path): path.append(u); visited[u] = True; minPath = 99999999; for v in g[u]: if v == p: continue; if visited[v]: minPath = min(minPath, len(path) - 1); else: minPath = self.dfs(v, u, g, visited, path); if minPath != 99999999: path.pop(); return minPath + 1; return 0; def solve(self, g): ans = float('inf'); for i in range(len(g)): for u in g[i]: if not visited[u]: visited[u] = True; path = []; dfs(u, -1, g, visited, path); ans = min(ans, minPath); return ans - 1 if ans != float('inf') else -1;
solutions.cpp: #include <vector> #include <iostream> using namespace std; class Solution { public: int dfs(int u, int p, vector<vector<int>>& g, vector<bool>& visited, vector<int>& path) { path.push_back(u); visited[u] = true; int minPath = 99999999; for (int v : g[u]) { if (v == p) continue; if (visited[v]) { minPath = min(minPath, path.size() - 1); } else { minPath = dfs(v, u, g, visited, path); path.pop_back(); } } if (minPath != 99999999) { return minPath + 1; } return 0; } int solve(vector<vector<int>>& g) { vector<bool> visited(g.size(), false); int ans = 99999999; for (int i = 0; i < g.size(); i++) { for (int u : g[i]) { visited[u] = true; vector<int> path; dfs(u, -1, g, visited, path); ans = min(ans, minPath); } } if (ans != 99999999) return ans - 1; return -1; } }; int main() { Solution solution; // Read input from std::cin ... }
